response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASUREMENT OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE. IT IS A KEY FACTOR IN MANY PHYSICAL PROCESSES AND REACTIONS. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SYSTEM CAN AFFECT THE RATE OF CHEMICAL REACTIONS. WHEN A SUBSTANCE IS HEATED, ITS TEMPERATURE INCREASES. THE TEMPERATURE OF A MATERIAL CAN CHANGE DURING PHASE TRANSFORMATIONS. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SOLUTION CAN BE MEASURED USING A TEMPERATURE SENSOR. THE TEMPERATURE OF A MATERIAL CAN AFFECT ITS PHYSICAL PROPERTIES. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SYSTEM CAN BE CHANGED BY ADDING OR REMOVING HEAT. THE TEMPERATURE OF A MATERIAL CAN BE USED TO DETERMINE ITS STATE OF MATTER. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SYSTEM CAN BE MEASURED USING A THERMOMETER. THE TEMPERATURE OF A MATERIAL CAN BE AFFECTED BY ITS ENVIRONMENT.
